description_long: This object is a pine cone, characterized by its roughly ovoid shape and layered, woody scales that spiral outward from a central core. The scales are thick, rounded, and tightly packed, with a slightly rough and matte surface texture. The coloration is predominantly a muted brown with lighter, almost grayish highlights on the tips of the scales, suggesting some weathering or exposure to the elements. The base of the cone is slightly irregular, with some scales appearing chipped or broken, typical of natural tree debris found on the ground. The overall structure is solid and dense, with no visible leaves or bark, and the scales provide a rugged, tactile surface. This pine cone would commonly be found as debris in urban parks, sidewalks, or near street trees.
description: A brown, ovoid pine cone with tightly packed, rounded woody scales and a rough, matte surface. The tips of the scales are lighter, giving a weathered appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full, rounded face of the pine cone with the central core and layered scales clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the dense, overlapping arrangement of the scales and the slightly irregular base.
description_view_2: Angled view emphasizing the compactness of the scales and the subtle color variations on their tips.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the back curvature and the consistent pattern of the scales around the cone.
